title: Section 68 Addition on Sale of Previously Disclosed Shares Deleted to Avoid Double Taxation: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi held Section 68 inapplicable where shares were disclosed in an earlier year and sale proceeds were already offered as income, avoiding double taxation. The post Section 68 Addition on Sale of Previously Disclosed Shares Deleted to Avoid Double Taxation: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that an addition under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act was inapplicable where shares were previously disclosed and sale proceeds offered as income. This decision by the Indian tax tribunal prevents double taxation on share sales.

title: Captive Service Provider Entitled to Risk Adjustment; Nil ALP for Proven Intra-Group Services Rejected: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi held that documentary evidence established receipt of intra-group administrative support services and that the 5% markup was at arm's length. The transfer pricing adjustment was accordingly deleted. The post Captive Service Provider Entitled to Risk Adjustment; Nil ALP for Proven Intra-Group Services Rejected: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that a captive service provider was entitled to a risk adjustment, rejecting the tax authority's attempt to apply a nil arm's length price for proven intra-group administrative support services. The tribunal accepted a 5% markup as arm's length, leading to the deletion of the transfer pricing adjustment.

title: DCF Share Valuation Accepted as AO Could Not Replace Prescribed Method with NAV
summary: ITAT held that an Assessing Officer cannot substitute the DCF method chosen under Rule 11UA with the NAV method without legal justification. The post DCF Share Valuation Accepted as AO Could Not Replace Prescribed Method with NAV appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that an Assessing Officer cannot arbitrarily substitute the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method for share valuation chosen under Rule 11UA with the Net Asset Value (NAV) method. This decision by the Indian tax tribunal affirms the taxpayer's right to use prescribed valuation methodologies.

ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Mumbai deleted a significant ₹572.83 Crore transfer pricing adjustment against Vodafone Idea concerning brand royalty payments. This ruling clarifies the arm's length nature of intra-group brand usage fees in India.

title: Functionally dissimilar entities, entrepreneurs, and product developers were invalid comparables for captive IT service providers
summary: Transfer pricing principles dictate that a captive, risk-mitigated service provider could not be benchmarked against full-fledged, risk-bearing entrepreneurs, companies owning substantial intellectual property, or those generating revenue through software products. The post Functionally dissimilar entities, entrepreneurs, and product developers were invalid comparables for captive IT service providers appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This TaxGuru article, likely summarizing a recent judicial pronouncement from India, asserts that functionally dissimilar entities, such as entrepreneurs or product developers, are invalid comparables for captive, risk-mitigated IT service providers. It reinforces the importance of precise functional and risk analysis in comparability benchmarking.

title: TP Addition Deleted as Management Support Services Proved Genuine
summary: The ITAT Pune held that detailed agreements, invoices and supporting records established that management support services were actually rendered by the Associated Enterprise. It ruled that the payment was at arm's length and deleted the entire transfer pricing adjustment. The post TP Addition Deleted as Management Support Services Proved Genuine appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Pune deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that management support services provided by an Associated Enterprise were genuine and at arm's length. The decision was based on detailed agreements, invoices, and supporting records proving the services were actually rendered.

title: ITAT Delhi Excludes TPO Comparables for Functional Dissimilarity
summary: ITAT excluded EDCIL, Just Dial, Info Edge and India Exposition Mart as transfer pricing comparables due to functional differences and restored one comparable for verification. The post ITAT Delhi Excludes TPO Comparables for Functional Dissimilarity appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi excluded several comparables, including EDCIL, Just Dial, Info Edge, and India Exposition Mart, from transfer pricing benchmarking due to functional dissimilarities. The tribunal also directed verification for one restored comparable, emphasizing the critical role of strict comparability analysis.

title: Royalty TP Adjustment deleted as TNMM Accepted as Most Appropriate Method
summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad held that royalty payments should continue to be benchmarked under TNMM by following earlier decisions in the assessees own case. It found no reason to replace TNMM with CUP where identical facts had already been decided. The Department's transfer pricing challenge was therefore dismissed. The post Royalty TP Adjustment deleted as TNMM Accepted as Most Appropriate Method appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Ahmedabad deleted a transfer pricing adjustment on royalty payments, affirming the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) as the Most Appropriate Method. The tribunal rejected the tax department's attempt to apply CUP, citing consistency with previous decisions on identical facts for the assessee.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 56(2)(viib) Addition as AO Changed Valuation Method Contrary to Rule 11UA
summary: ITAT held that the AO could not change the valuation method adopted under Rule 11UA and deleted the addition under Section 56(2)(viib). The post ITAT Deletes Section 56(2)(viib) Addition as AO Changed Valuation Method Contrary to Rule 11UA appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT deleted an addition made under Section 56(2)(viib), ruling that the Assessing Officer could not unilaterally change the valuation method adopted by the assessee under Rule 11UA. title: Bengaluru ITAT: Apartment Owners’ Welfare Association Not Taxable at Maximum Marginal Rate
summary: ITAT held that a society registered under the Karnataka Societies Registration Act cannot be taxed at the maximum marginal rate under Section 167B. The post Bengaluru ITAT: Apartment Owners’ Welfare Association Not Taxable at Maximum Marginal Rate appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bengaluru ITAT ruled that an apartment owners' welfare association, registered under the Karnataka Societies Registration Act, cannot be taxed at the maximum marginal rate under Section 167B. This decision clarifies the tax treatment for specific non-profit associations in India.

title: Section 50CA Valuation Rejected as Rule 11UA Ignored Unrealizable Assets: ITAT Chennai
summary: ITAT Chennai held that unrealizable assets of a company under insolvency could not be included while computing fair market value under Rule 11UA. The Tribunal directed recomputation using the NCLT auction price instead of the artificial valuation adopted by the AO. The post Section 50CA Valuation Rejected as Rule 11UA Ignored Unrealizable Assets: ITAT Chennai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Chennai rejected a Section 50CA valuation that ignored unrealizable assets of a company under insolvency, ruling that Rule 11UA should reflect the fair market value. The tribunal directed recomputation using the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) auction price, rather than the tax officer's initial artificial valuation.

title: CIT(A) Can Remand Best Judgment Reassessment Due to Assessee’s Non-Compliance: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi held that the CIT(A) validly remanded a best judgment reassessment after repeated non-compliance by the assessee. The Tribunal ruled that the proviso to Section 251(1)(a) empowered the appellate authority to order a fresh reassessment. The post CIT(A) Can Remand Best Judgment Reassessment Due to Assessee’s Non-Compliance: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi affirmed that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) validly remanded a best judgment reassessment in a case of repeated non-compliance by the assessee. The tribunal confirmed the appellate authority's power under Section 251(1)(a) to order a fresh reassessment.

title: TP Adjustment Set Aside for Failure to Prove Profit Shifting Under Section 80-IA(10)
summary: The ITAT held that a transfer pricing adjustment under Section 80-IA(10) cannot be sustained without proving the statutory conditions, including close connection, arranged business transactions, and more than ordinary profits. The Tribunal deleted the adjustment for lack of foundational evidence. The post TP Adjustment Set Aside for Failure to Prove Profit Shifting Under Section 80-IA(10) appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that a transfer pricing adjustment under India's Section 80-IA(10) cannot be sustained without explicit proof of profit shifting, including close connection and transactions yielding more than ordinary profits. The Tribunal deleted the adjustment due to the tax authorities' failure to establish the foundational statutory conditions.

title: ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as Earlier Coordinate Bench Ruling Was Binding
summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment of ₹61.22 crore after finding the facts identical to an earlier assessment year. It held that a pending High Court appeal does not dilute the binding nature of an unreversed Tribunal decision. The post ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as Earlier Coordinate Bench Ruling Was Binding appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment of ₹61.22 crore, asserting that a previous coordinate bench ruling with identical facts for an earlier assessment year was binding. The Tribunal emphasized that a pending High Court appeal does not diminish the precedential value of an unreversed Tribunal decision.

title: Transfer Pricing Addition Deleted Because ALP Was Fixed at Nil Without Prescribed Method
summary: ITAT ruled that determining the arm's length price of management service fees at Nil without following a prescribed transfer pricing methodology was not sustainable. It upheld the CIT(A)'s order deleting both the management fee and manufacturing segment adjustments. The post Transfer Pricing Addition Deleted Because ALP Was Fixed at Nil Without Prescribed Method appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that determining the arm's length price for management service fees at Nil without adhering to a prescribed transfer pricing methodology is unsustainable. The Tribunal upheld the CIT(A)'s decision to delete the transfer pricing additions related to both the management fee and the manufacturing segment.

title: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as TPO Used Ad Hoc Method Instead of Section 92C
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that transfer pricing adjustments for intra-group services were unsustainable because the TPO determined the arms length price without applying any prescribed method under Section 92C. It directed deletion of the adjustments on this technical ground. The post ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as TPO Used Ad Hoc Method Instead of Section 92C appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted transfer pricing adjustments for intra-group services, ruling that the Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) failed to apply any prescribed method under Section 92C to determine the arm's length price. The Tribunal directed the deletion of the adjustments on the technical grounds of improper methodological application.

summary: The Tribunal ruled that ignoring the assessees additional evidence, including the registered valuers report, vitiated the appellate proceedings.
